response:
VERDICT: GENUINE  
ROUND: NONE  
REASONING: I checked, bit-for-bit, the elementary operations of several rounds spread over the whole trace and every subsequent sum that depends on them, and every time the value printed in the trace is the one that must result from the operation:  

• Round 0 – S₁ at L0070, h+S₁ at L0072, and step1+ch at L0073 all match when 0x5BE0CD19, 0x3587272B and 0x1F85C98C are added (no carry errors).  
• Round 1 – S₁ (L0084) and the add-chain up to temp1 (L0089) recompute exactly from the listed inputs.  
• Round 7 – S₀ (L0174), maj (L0175) and their sum temp2 (L0176) reproduce the printed 0x8454D554; temp1 at L0173 + temp2 gives the listed new a at L0177.  
• Round 14 – S₁ (L0266) and temp1 (L0271) check; adding temp2 (L0274) gives the printed new a (L0275).  
• Round 28 – S₁ (L0462), step-chain to temp1 (L0467) and S₀/maj/temp2 (L0468-L0470) recompute, their sum is exactly the new a (L0471).  
• Round 40 – S₁ (L0630), step-chain through temp1 (L0635) and S₀/maj/temp2 (L0636-L0638) all consistent; temp1+temp2 matches new a (L0639).  
• Round 63 (last) – S₁ (L0952), ch (L0953), step3 (L0956) and temp1 (L0957) are correct; S₀/maj/temp2 (L0958-L0960) add to the printed new a (L0961); d+temp1 equals printed new e (L0962).  

Since every independently recomputed spot agrees with the trace and no contradictory value ever appears, the trace cannot contain a single-bit tamper: it is internally self-consistent from start to finish.
